high.title: U.S.-Iran War Impact: Container Shipping Rates Surge as Major Routes Disrupted
high.summary:
Global container shipping giants are rerouting vessels away from the Persian Gulf amid escalating U.S.-Israel military conflict with Iran, threatening to disrupt global trade and drive up spot shipping rates. MSC has suspended Middle East cargo bookings, while Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd have halted all Strait of Hormuz transits. Hapag-Lloyd imposed a $1,500 war risk surcharge per 20-foot container. The crisis could trigger port congestion elsewhere as cargo reroutes, similar to previous Red Sea disruptions. Taiwan's container trio (Evergreen 2603, Yang Ming 2609, Wan Hai 2615) all suspended Red Sea operations and are closely monitoring developments. This represents a significant setback to 2026 plans for full Suez Canal resumption after Yemen Houthi attacks forced African detours since December 2023.

**Taiwan Dollar and Energy Security Under Pressure from Middle East Crisis**
📂 Section: 金融 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/03 03:08:18
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5612/9355202?from=edn_subcatelist_cate

Qatar's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz threatens Taiwan's energy security, with Qatar being Taiwan's largest LNG import source. LNG spot prices are surging, potentially driving up electricity costs and impacting multiple industries including machinery, ceramics, and retail. Taiwan Machine Tool Association chairman warns against further gas price volatility as industries struggle with previous 70% gas price increases and 60% electricity hikes over two years. The ceramics industry faces severe cost pressures and urges government to halt further industrial gas price increases. Retail operators express concern over electricity cost impacts on operations, particularly for 24/7 convenience stores.

**TSMC's After-Hours Trading Hits Record NT$2,190 - Points to 37,000 Target**
📂 Section: 證券 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/03 03:01:38
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5607/9355206?from=edn_subcatelist_cate

TSMC's after-hours block trading reached a historic high of NT$2,190 per share, marking the first time above NT$2,100. This suggests potential upside of 1,711 points for the TAIEX, targeting 36,806 points and approaching the 37,000 milestone. Despite foreign institutional selling of NT$38.5 billion in TSMC on March 2, domestic institutions continued accumulating, with investment trusts buying for 16 consecutive days (total NT$36.5 billion) and proprietary traders buying for 6 days (total NT$13.6 billion). The block trading involved 15 transactions totaling 1,937 shares worth NT$3.8 billion, with weighted average price of NT$1,970.95, slightly below the closing price of NT$1,975.

medium.title: Foreign Investors Heavily Sell Taiwan Stocks Amid Geopolitical Risks
medium.summary:
Taiwan stocks staged a V-shaped recovery despite opening down 809 points due to U.S.-Iran conflict, with support from national team and investment trusts limiting final decline to 319 points (-0.9%). Foreign investors sold NT$43.2 billion total, with institutional selling of NT$38.98 billion leading the outflows. However, investment trusts bought NT$8.32 billion while government-linked brokerages purchased NT$9.93 billion, and major life insurers bought NT$20.06 billion, providing crucial support.

**MediaTek and Qualcomm Battle for 6G Leadership at MWC 2026**
📂 Section: 金融 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/03 02:32:25
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5612/9355083?from=edn_maintab_cate

At MWC 2026, MediaTek and Qualcomm are showcasing competing 6G technologies. Qualcomm announced partnerships to accelerate 6G development, targeting commercial system delivery from 2029, with ASUS listed among cooperation partners. MediaTek demonstrated breakthrough "6G wireless access interoperability" achievements and AI-enhanced transmission technologies. Both companies' CEOs will deliver keynote speeches on AI-native 6G architectures, highlighting Taiwan's key role in next-generation wireless infrastructure.

**Taiwan Life Insurers Benefit from Geopolitical Flight to Quality**
📂 Section: 證券 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/03 03:04:57
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5607/9355198?from=edn_maintab_cate

Major Taiwan life insurers purchased NT$20.06 billion of stocks during market turmoil, taking advantage of lower prices amid foreign selling. TSMC's fractional share trading surged to NT$12.13 billion, the first time exceeding NT$10 billion this year and ranking as the fourth-highest in history. This "ant moving elephant" buying pattern from retail investors provided crucial market support, demonstrating continued confidence in Taiwan's tech leadership despite geopolitical uncertainties.

**Hon Hai Group's AI Server Revenue Surges Over 300% in 2025**
📂 Section: 金融 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/02 04:35:05
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5612/9352118?from=edn_subcatelist_cate

Hon Hai is preparing for NVIDIA's GTC conference to showcase Vera Rubin integrated rack solutions. Industrial Fulian reported Q4 cloud service provider AI server revenue grew over 50% quarter-over-quarter and over 550% year-over-year, with full-year 2025 AI server revenue surging over 300%. The company has established comprehensive L1-L12 manufacturing capabilities, positioning for next-generation AI infrastructure opportunities.

**Qatar Energy Suspends LNG Production After Iranian Drone Attacks**
📂 Section: 產經 | 📅 Date: 2026/03/02 20:16:20
🔗 https://money.udn.com/money/story/5599/9354845

Qatar Energy announced suspension of LNG and related product production due to Iranian drone attacks on Ras Laffan and Mesaieed facilities. As the world's second-largest LNG supplier and Taiwan's biggest LNG import source, this disruption threatens global energy security. Goldman Sachs estimates Asian LNG spot prices could surge 130% to $25/MMBtu if Strait of Hormuz remains closed for a month. Taiwan's government has activated energy contingency measures and coal backup capacity.
